disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The behavioural escalation began abruptly straight after a minor change to the evening routine (dinner about 15 minutes late), with no preceding fall, head knock or injury.
trigger_question_examples: What was happening just before this started?
Was there anything that set him off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It kicked off the moment dinner was late — about 15 minutes. There was no fall or bump, nothing like that. Just the change to his routine and he lost it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Over the past three hours the child has made repeated attempts to unlock the front door and run out toward a busy road, and the carer cannot physically contain him.
trigger_question_examples: Is he trying to leave or get outside?
Is he safe where he is right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He keeps going for the front door to get out to the street. I've had to push a couch against it. I can't hold him — he's too strong and I'm worn out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He has struck the carer with a chair and remains physically aggressive; the sole carer is exhausted and unable to keep him safe.
trigger_question_examples: Is he lashing out or hurting anyone?
Are you able to keep both of you safe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He hit me with a chair. He's still lashing out and I honestly can't keep him or myself safe much longer.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He has no fever, is not pulling at his ears or jaw, has no obvious pain, rash or injury, and there was no fall or knock before this began.
trigger_question_examples: Does he seem to be in any pain — ears, teeth, tummy?
Any fever, rash, vomiting, or a knock to the head today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that I can tell, he's not grabbing his ears or face, no rash, and he didn't fall or hit his head. It really was just the routine changing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: His usual calming strategies and his as-needed melatonin have not helped, and his regular morning medication was already given today.
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything to calm him?
What medications has he had today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I gave him his usual morning medicine and I tried his melatonin, but nothing has touched it. All his usual calming things aren't working.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He has behavioural meltdowns at times, but the carer has never seen one this severe or lasting this long.
trigger_question_examples: Is this normal for him, or is it different this time?
Has he ever been like this before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He does have meltdowns, but never like this — never this bad and never for this long. That's why I'm so worried.
